definition: to throw all together into confusion, to confound, disturb, trouble , Lat. conturbare, (Iliad by Homer), attic:—;Pass., αἰθὴρ πόντῳ ξυντετάρακται air is confounded with  sea, (Aeschulus Tragicus): to be thrown into confusion , of soldiers, (Thucydides); of social order, (Sophocles Tragicus); metaphorically of persons, to be confounded, greatly troubled, much distressed , (Herdotus Historicus) (ML)
